content.explanation: Creating indexes in SQL is essential for improving query performance on large tables. Indexes are created using the CREATE INDEX statement and can be single-column, multi-column (composite), or unique indexes. The syntax varies slightly between databases but follows similar patterns. Indexes should be created on columns frequently used in WHERE clauses, JOIN conditions, and ORDER BY clauses. However, indexes have trade-offs: they improve read performance but slow down write operations (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) and consume additional storage. Understanding when and how to create indexes is crucial for database performance tuning. Different index types include B-Tree (default), Hash (for equality), Bitmap (for low-cardinality), and Full-text (for text search).
